title: What you should know before you apply: Application for a Trust Account Number  - Canada.ca
content:
## What you should know before you apply
A trustee can apply for a trust account number. The trustee includes a filer, a custodian, an executor, an administrator, or a liquidator who owns or controls property for another person.
This application also applies to a non-resident trust electing to file an income tax return under section 216 of the Income Tax Act. For more information, please see the T4144 : Income Tax Guide for Electing under Section 216 .

### What you need before you start
You need to know your trust name, contact information, and trust type .
You will also need to provide a signed copy of the trust document or the last will and testament .
If any of the legal representatives named in the last will and testament are deceased, you need to include a copy of their death certificate. If a representative no longer wants to act as a representative, we need a letter of renunciation from that representative.
For more information about representatives, go to Representative authorization .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
